title: Wrld Side Quests — AR/AR-game concept where post-COVID youth seek IRL
html:
Wrld Side Quests — AR/AR-game concept where post-COVID youth seek IRL

Source: 2024-07-12 Apple Note. The first dedicated PRD-style writeup of Side Quest App — an idea that recurs across the 2024 ideas list and the mega notebook.

TL;DR

  • Thesis: post-COVID young people who experienced isolation are now seeking IRL — running clubs, "simple/stupid phones", traveling. Side Quest App rides that wave.
  • Concept: real-wrld quests integrated with virtual world, varying difficulty levels = different XP. "Quests with the boys sounds sick."
  • Quest authoring — open question: "Us the Creator" (centralized) vs geocache-style community-contributed (with Creator approval). How to avoid dangerous/false quests is the moderation problem.
  • Example quest: hike to top of Table Rock → AR camera reveals an artifact → battle starts (e.g., evil tree). Or riddle-puzzle path to artifact.
  • Hardest part = AR, most important = social aspect.
  • Direct lineage: same idea reappears in 2024 12 28 ideas list as one bullet, and in 2024 07 04 questions as a one-liner.

Pattern observed

This is the most fully-formed pre-Brotein/pre-Rankett app concept in the corpus. Notable that the surface is play (a game) but the underlying thesis is the post COVID IRL hunger — same insight that animates Brotein's "WeRun" group share and IRL Lore's journal-as-life-narrative pivot.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir ever start prototyping this? (No SwiftUI code surfaced.)
  • The "Us the Creator" framing implies a hierarchy — was this meant to be a startup or a community DAO?
  • Why doesn't Side Quest App appear in the stealth-apps roster? Was it discarded?
